items.title: Berman: Rodman deal shows new rule is working
items.summary: NWSL commissioner Jessica Berman cited Trinity Rodman's new deal as evidence that the league's new High Impact Player is working as intended: to retain and attract top players.

items.title: Infantino: Resale will drive up WC ticket prices
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino said that many fans who have applied for tickets for the 2026 World Cup will be looking to sell them for profit on resale sites, thus driving up the cost of ticket prices.

items.title: German official calls for World Cup boycott talks
items.summary: A German soccer federation executive committee member says it's time to consider a World Cup boycott because of the actions of U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: ADO-coach hekelt spelopvatting Vitesse: 'Wij houden er niet van'
items.summary: ADO Den Haag-coach Robin Peter heeft niet genoten van de wedstrijd tegen Vitesse. Niet alleen door het resultaat, maar de Duitser stoorde zich ook aan het spel van de Arnhemmers.

items.title: Inter komt keepersfout al in eerste helft te boven tegen promovendus Pisa
items.summary: Internazionale heeft vrijdagavond een eenvoudige zege geboekt op promovendus Pisa. De koploper van Italië schrok in de eerste helft nog wel even na een fout van doelman Yann Sommer, maar liep daarna uit naar een ruime zege.

items.title: Supporters geven teleurstellende Hamburgse derby kleur
items.summary: Dit seizoen staat de Hamburgse derby voor het eerst in lange tijd weer op het Bundesliga-speelschema. Echt enthousiast werden de spelers van Sankt Pauli en Hamburger SV daar niet van, want de burenruzie was zaterdag weinig om over naar huis te schrijven. Aan de fans van de ploegen lag dat niet.

items.title: Derde nederlaag op rij voor ADO: Cambuur krijgt koploper in het vizier
items.summary: ADO Den Haag heeft vrijdagavond zijn derde nederlaag op rij geleden in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie. Na Jong PSV en SC Cambuur was Vitesse te sterk voor de koploper.

items.title: Barcola voorkomt nieuwe averij voor PSG in Franse titelstrijd
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain leek vrijdag lang op weg naar nieuw puntenverlies in de Franse titelstrijd. Op bezoek in Auxerre had de Parijse grootmacht het lastig, maar in de slotfase voorkwam Bradley Barcola een sof.

items.title: England fans' World Cup arrest record shows Infantino joke inaccurate
items.summary: Did Fifa president Gianni Infantino lean into stereotypes about England fans being arrested at the World Cup?

items.summary: News from Friday’s press conferences, including Chelsea, Manchester United and Aston Villa Continue reading...

items.title: Deadly deliveries and controlled chaos: how Arsenal became set-piece masters
items.summary: Mikel Arteta’s side have scored 19 goals from corners this season. Why are their set pieces so effective and can they be stopped? Set pieces are dominating the Premier League this season, with almost 30% of goals coming from corners, free-kicks, penalties or long throws. The leaders, Arsenal, are kings of the dead ball, scoring 17 of their 40 league goals from set pieces (including penalties). But what makes Mikel Arteta’s side so effective in these areas, and what can opponents do to stop them? The data provides some answers. Continue reading...

items.title: Smotherman collapses at weigh-in for UFC 324 as fight cancelled
items.summary: A fight at UFC 324 has been cancelled after American fighter Cameron Smotherman collapsed during the weigh-in on Friday.

items.title: Premier League Power Rankings: Rating all 20 teams on performances, not results
items.summary: In the debut of ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings, a look at all 20 teams, including why Liverpool, Chelsea and Tottenham are better than the table suggests.

items.title: Keep, Dump or Extend: How far will Chelsea go to back Liam Rosenior?
items.summary: Will Chelsea add cover at center back in January to support their new coach?

items.title: Euros abuse made me not want to leave hotel - Carter
items.summary: England defender Jess Carter says abuse of female footballers is getting worse and that she has had to step back from social media after receiving racist messages during Euro 2025.

items.title: Palmer 'very, very happy' at Chelsea, says Rosenior
items.summary: Reports suggest Chelsea forward Cole Palmer is homesick amid links to Manchester United.

items.title: Glasner '100% committed' to Palace for rest of season
items.summary: Oliver Glasner says he is "100% committed" to Crystal Palace for the remainder of his time as manager of the club.

items.title: Hamilton 'massively excited' for 'new beginning' in F1 2026
items.summary: Lewis Hamilton says he is "massively excited" for "new beginnings" heading into Formula 1's latest rules era after he drove Ferrari's 2026 car for the first time.

items.title: Borthwick: England's prop crisis is disruptive - but we'll attack Six Nations
items.summary: England head coach Steve Borthwick has conceded to Sky Sports his squad's prop injury crisis is "unfortunate and disruptive" but says they will "attack the tournament" in search of a first Six Nations title since 2020.
